request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will the SEC officially classify Ether as a security by 2026-12-31?
Kind: active
Timeline (20 events):
- 2026-05-18 [background] Rescission of Policy Regarding Denials in Settlements of Enforcement Actions — Rescinding the SEC’s denial-in-settlement policy could modestly increase chance enforcement yields a definitive finding that ETH is a security.
- 2026-05-13 [background] CME | Expansion of the Trading and ClearingHours for all Cryptocurrency Futures and Options on Futures Contracts. | [1] — CFTC expanding crypto futures/clearing hours reinforces commodity-market treatment of crypto, slightly reducing likelihood SEC will classify ETH as a security.
- 2026-04-16 [background] COIN | Addition of a new Exchange functionality… - 04/16/2026 — CFTC action for Coinbase derivatives reinforces CFTC/commodity framing of ETH, modestly lowering probability the SEC will classify ETH as a security.
- 2026-04-14 [background] CBOT | Modifications to the Eris Block… - 04/14/2026 — CFTC filing on CBOT rule changes doesn't determine SEC classification; at best it's a minor signal favoring commodity treatment, slightly lowering YES odds.
- 2026-04-13 [background] Staff Statement Regarding Broker-Dealer Registration of Certain User Interfaces Utilized to Prepare Transactions in Crypto Asset Securities — SEC staff guidance labeling certain tokens as 'crypto asset securities' and pushing broker-dealer registration for interfaces raises odds ETH could be treated as a security.
- 2026-04-08 [C] SEC Appoints David Woodcock as Director of the Division of Enforcement — Appointment of a new Enforcement Director modestly raises chance/timing of SEC civil actions that could produce a judgment classifying ETH as a security.
- 2026-03-23 [B] 91 FR 13714 - 3/23/2026 - Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets (PDF) — SEC Federal Register notice on applying securities laws to crypto assets raises odds ETH will be treated as a security by signaling active rulemaking.
- 2026-03-23 [B] 91 FR 13714 - 3/23/2026 - Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets — SEC Federal Register item on applying federal securities laws to crypto signals substantive rulemaking that raises odds ETH is a security.
- 2026-03-18 [background] Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets and Certain Transactions Involving Crypto Assets Corporation Finance — An SEC Corporation Finance publication on applying federal securities laws to crypto assets raises the chance ETH will be treated as a security, but is not a binding order.
- 2026-03-16 [background] 91 FR 12516 — CFTC Federal Register item signals CFTC activity on ETH/crypto, which indirectly reduces odds of an SEC security finding and may slow SEC action.
- 2026-02-17 [background] 91 FR 7264 — A CFTC Federal Register action could signal the CFTC treating ETH as a commodity, modestly lowering odds the SEC will formally call ETH a security, but content is uncertain.
- 2026-02-12 [background] CFE Cboe Ether Continuous Futures Lead Market Maker Program Amendment [2] — CFTC approval/amendment of Cboe Ether futures program reinforces commodity framing of ETH, slightly lowering SEC-security odds.
- 2026-02-03 [background] ICE US New Cryptocurrency Futures Market Maker Program — A CFTC program supporting crypto futures implies continued commodity-market support for crypto, slightly lowering odds SEC will classify ETH as a security (indirect).
- 2026-01-29 [background] COIN | Modifications to the Perp Style Futures Market Maker Program — CFTC tweaks to perp-futures program (with Coinbase) signal continued CFTC oversight of crypto derivatives, slightly lowering odds SEC will classify ETH as a security.
- 2026-01-22 [background] ICE US — CFTC/ICE Futures record is not an SEC action; CFTC involvement implies commodity treatment of ETH, slightly reducing chance the SEC will formally deem ETH a security.
- 2025-12-23 [background] CME Modifications to the Micro Bitcoin Euro and Micro Ether Euro Futures Market Maker Program — CFTC action approving/modifying CME micro Ether futures MM program reinforces Ether's treatment in derivatives markets (commodity-regulator), modestly lowering SEC-security odds.
- 2025-12-23 [background] CME Modifications to the Ether/Bitcoin Ratio Futures Market Maker Program — CME/CFTC changes to Ether futures reinforce commodity-market treatment of ETH, slightly lowering odds the SEC will classify ETH as a security.
- 2025-10-01 [background] Pending Administrative Proceedings — Announces SEC pending administrative proceedings but gives no Ether-specific details, slightly raising chance of agency action but not materially altering ETH-as-security odds.
- 2025-09-19 [B] _Federal Register_ version — SEC Federal Register publication on 2025-09-19 likely represents a final rule text — strongly affects whether ETH is formally classified and speeds resolution.
- 2025-09-10 [background] 90 FR 43586 — CFTC Federal Register notice signals agency action under the Commodity Exchange Act; a CFTC view that ETH is a commodity reduces the chance the SEC will deem it a security.

response.text: Throughout 2025-26, SEC signals (91 FR 13714, CorpFin guidance, broker-dealer UI statement, new Enforcement Director, and rescission of the denial-in-settlement policy) raise the prospect of an ETH-as-security finding. In parallel, broad CFTC/CME/Cboe actions expand ETH derivatives and implicitly affirm commodity treatment, tempering odds. No official SEC classification yet; outcome hinges on pending SEC rulemaking/enforcement before 2026-12-31.
